excerpt: Through this spiritual sustenance, believers engage in the perpetual acquisition of knowledge of the Imam.
source_location_hint: On the continuation of knowledge after death and the ranks of the believers

excerpt: The Imam's son—he who is destined to become Imam—is the nūr (light), and he mirrors the believers' praise.
source_location_hint: On the succession of Imams and the transmission of doctrine

excerpt: None among the limits of the summons possessed the capacity to become Imam while advancing through the stations, save the Imam's son alone.
source_location_hint: On the unique qualification of the Imam's successor

excerpt: The Imam is the khalīfa (successor) and hijāb (veil) of ʿAyn al-ʿAẓīma. Without Allah there exists no capacity nor strength.
source_location_hint: On the supreme rank of the Imam and the transmission of power

excerpt: The Throne Verse is so exalted that none recites it except the Messenger of Allah, the sincere seeker, and the believer whom Allah has tested with faith.
source_location_hint: On the merit and recitation of the Throne Verse

excerpt: The Essence of 'Lā Ilāha Illā Huwa' bears the numeric value of 110, which corresponds to the name ʿAlī.
source_location_hint: On the numerical correspondences in the names of Allah and the Imams

excerpt: The Essence has never been heedless in any moment of the past, nor shall It ever be negligent in any future time.
source_location_hint: On the eternal vigilance and perfection of the Divine Essence

excerpt: Just as the throne never tires from bearing weight, so too does the Essence never weary from the tremendous responsibility of the entire age.
source_location_hint: On the unfailing sustenance of the cosmos by the Imam

excerpt: Our higher rank is the master of our good; this is why the Qur'an declares we should long for death as the gateway to freedom.
source_location_hint: On the surrender of forms and the perfection of the self through death

excerpt: The abode of the Hereafter is the supreme realm where the form reaches its rest and perfection; one's higher rank is one's alone.
source_location_hint: On the Hereafter and the individuation of spiritual stations

excerpt: The Night of Might is the night of transmissions; wherever spiritual centers are established, a succession of transmissions is issued throughout this night.
source_location_hint: On the nocturnal dissemination of doctrine and hierarchy
